payment_terms: Equal monthly installments of $3,500 due on the 15th of each month, paid in advance. First and last payments prorated if applicable.
scope_summary: CSCG provides comprehensive professional management services for the North Shore Ice Arena facility, including full staffing, day-to-day operations, revenue collection, expense payment, maintenance, marketing, program development, and financial recordkeeping. CSCG has complete authority over daily operations subject to Owner-established policies and guidelines.
termination_for_cause: Upon payment default, non-defaulting party may suspend performance after 5 days written notice. If default not cured within 30 days of suspension notice or other default notice, non-defaulting party may terminate. All remedies at law available to non-defaulting party upon termination.
termination_for_convenience: Either party may terminate without cause upon 90 days written notice prior to the end of the then-current term (initial or any renewal year).
performance_obligations: Operate and maintain the Facility, its equipment, materials, and supplies
Fully staff the Facility with CSCG employees including a full-time manager acceptable to Owner
Collect all gross revenues and deposit into Owner's general account within one (1) business day of receipt
Pay all operating expenses from Owner's operating account by their due dates
Prepare and submit annual month-by-month line item operating budget at least 30 days prior to start of each fiscal/calendar year
Provide prior written notice to Owner whenever budgeted amounts are projected to be exceeded, including revised budget amounts
Submit adjusted deposit requirements to Owner by the 15th of the preceding month for subsequent month changes

Submit financial reports to Owner no less than 24 hours prior to each regularly scheduled monthly board meeting, including balance sheet, revenues/expenditures (budget to actual), cash flow statement, and general ledger
Provide information necessary for preparation and filing of annual tax returns
Maintain all financial records and make available for Owner inspection during normal business hours
Return all financial records to Owner upon termination and preparation of final financial statement
Conduct joint inventories of assets, equipment, and supplies as reasonably required by Owner
Obtain Owner prior approval before entering any vendor/service/rental contract with a term longer than one year
Obtain Owner prior approval before purchasing goods, equipment, or services in excess of $3,500
Copy Owner on all renewal and new contracts
Obtain Owner approval for repair and maintenance expenses exceeding $3,500
Notify Owner promptly after making emergency repairs without prior consent
Ensure all repairs and replacements are of quality at least equal to original work
Carry commercial general liability insurance with Owner named as additional insured
Carry Workers Compensation and Employers Liability insurance on all Facility personnel
Carry Employee Theft Insurance with limits and deductibles approved by Owner
Deliver all computer data compilations and financial records to Owner upon termination
Maintain confidentiality of all Owner information and operations
reporting_obligations: Monthly financial reports submitted no less than 24 hours before each Owner board meeting, including balance sheet, budget-to-actual revenue and expense statement, year-to-date and prior year comparisons, cash flow statement, and general ledger copy
Prior written notice to Owner whenever approved budget amounts are projected to be exceeded, with revised budget amounts
Adjusted operating account deposit requirements submitted to Owner by the 15th of the preceding month
Prompt notification to Owner following any emergency repairs made without prior Owner consent
Copies of all new and renewal contracts entered into on behalf of the Facility
board_approval_required: Annual operating budget approval required before start of each fiscal/calendar year (Article 5.1)
Any vendor, service, or user/rental occupancy contract with a term longer than one year (Article 8.1A)
Any purchase of goods, equipment, or services exceeding $3,500 (Article 8.1B)
Any repair or maintenance expense exceeding $3,500 (Article 9.1)
Insurance policy types, coverage amounts, deductible limits, and carrier selection for CSCG-carried policies (Article 12.3)
Number, size, and location of CSCG signage on or about the Facility (Schedule D)
Provision of sufficient operating funds, including initial two-month deposit and ongoing monthly replenishment (Article 6.4)
red_flags: CSCG is a signatory on Owner's operating account and controls disbursement of all Facility funds — significant financial control risk with limited apparent oversight mechanism beyond monthly reporting
Agreement dated December 1, 2007 with commencement August 1, 2008 — eight-month gap raises question of whether interim management arrangements exist or whether this agreement is still operative

Initial three-year term expired July 31, 2011; agreement has been auto-renewing on annual basis since then with no documented renegotiation of compensation or terms — $42,000 annual fee may be significantly outdated
Owner must maintain a two-month operating expense reserve in the account at all times, creating a potentially substantial ongoing capital obligation for Owner with no cap stated

One-year statute of limitations on arbitration claims (Article 15.1) is significantly shorter than typical statutory limitations periods and could disadvantage Owner in detecting and pursuing financial irregularities
Mandatory arbitration clause with express waiver of consequential and punitive damages limits Owner's remedies in event of CSCG misconduct or gross negligence
CSCG retains proprietary rights to all computer programs and software — Owner may face operational disruption and data access issues upon termination
No performance standards, KPIs, or benchmarks defined for CSCG management performance; 'complete authority over day to day operation' with limited objective accountability measures
Schedules B and C (personal property supplied by each party) appear blank or incomplete in the document, creating ambiguity over asset ownership and return obligations at termination
Article 14.3 default cure period is only 30 days, which may be insufficient for complex operational or financial disputes
No explicit cap on CSCG's ability to hire employees and set compensation at Facility expense, beyond general budget approval process
CSCG may place its own signage identifying itself as manager and leasing agent, potentially creating public-facing brand confusion or commitments
extraction_notes: Schedules B and C (Owner and CSCG personal property lists) are blank, preventing assessment of asset ownership obligations. Schedule A compensation terms are present but no escalation or CPI adjustment clause is visible — fee has apparently remained at $42,000 since 2008. Several article subsection numbers appear in the document (1.1, 2.1, 3.1, 4.1-4.4, 5.1-5.2, 6.1-6.4, etc.) but some subsection text appears truncated or missing between pages, particularly Articles 1, 4, and 6, suggesting possible missing content. Article 7.5 is referenced in Schedule B but Article 7 only numbers to 7.4 in the extracted text — possible missing subsection. No current amendment or addendum is included; given the agreement originated in 2007-2008 and has auto-renewed for approximately 15+ years, board should confirm whether any amendments exist and whether current operational terms have been formally updated. Legal review recommended for arbitration waiver provisions, financial control structure, and termination mechanics before any renewal or renegotiation.

Full AIA Agreement and all exhibits (A through F) should be reviewed and approved by the board prior to execution
red_flags: This document is an invoice only — the underlying AIA Agreement and all exhibits (A–F) are referenced but not included; key terms, warranties, liability limits, and termination rights are unknown
No payment schedule beyond the 30% down payment is visible; remaining 70% draw schedule and conditions are undocumented here
Vendor address is in Salix, PA — no Illinois contractor license or bonding information is visible on this document
No warranty terms, defect liability period, or performance guarantees are stated in this invoice
No lien waiver provisions are referenced, which is a significant risk for a $620,919 construction project
No insurance certificate or indemnification terms are visible; review of Exhibit A General Conditions is critical
Due on receipt terms for a $186,275.70 payment on a capital project is aggressive and warrants scrutiny
extraction_notes: This document is a single-page invoice only, not the underlying contract. The AIA Agreement, Exhibit A (General Conditions), Exhibit B (Scope of Work and Exclusions), Exhibit C (Schedule of Values), Exhibit D (Original Sales Proposal), Exhibit E (Pre-Mobilization Checklist), and Exhibit F (Daily Supervision Sign-Off Sheet) are all referenced but not provided. Critical commercial and legal terms — including warranties, insurance requirements, indemnification, lien waivers, change order procedures, termination rights, and the full payment draw schedule — cannot be extracted without those documents. Board and legal review of the full AIA Agreement package is strongly recommended before the down payment is released.

reporting_obligations: No formal reporting obligations identified in this invoice document

red_flags: Rental insurance explicitly declined — NSIA assumes full risk of loss or damage to the rented 300-ton chiller with no stated value of the unit disclosed on this invoice
No termination, cancellation, or early return provisions visible in this document; full contract terms and exclusions are referenced but not included

Signatory (Mike LaPato, 1-16-25) authority to bind NSIA to a $64,311.14 expenditure plus ongoing monthly obligations is not confirmed in this document
Invoice references 'SEE PROPOSAL FOR FULL SCOPE AND EXCLUSIONS' but no proposal is attached — key contractual terms, exclusions, and liability caps are unknown from this document alone
No governing law, dispute resolution, or liability limitation clauses are visible; these likely exist in the referenced proposal
Round-trip freight of $4,000.00 is bundled into this invoice but timing and conditions of return/removal are not defined here
extraction_notes: This document is a single-page invoice only, not a formal rental agreement or contract. The invoice references a separate proposal (CHI 25-11321977) containing full scope and exclusions that was not provided for review. Critical commercial terms including cancellation rights, liability allocation, damage responsibility, insurance requirements, and renewal notice obligations cannot be assessed without the underlying proposal and any signed rental agreement. Legal review of the complete proposal and contract documents is strongly recommended before the next billing cycle. Annual value estimated by annualizing the $19,665.00 recurring 28-day rate (x12 periods).

payment_terms: Payment due by 1/13/2026 for current billing period 11/19/2025-12/18/2025; late payments accrue simple interest at 1.5% per month; non-payment may result in contract termination
scope_summary: ENGIE Resources supplies fixed-price electricity to North Shore Ice Arena at 2111 Founders Dr, Northbrook IL 60062 under a supply contract expiring November 2026. The bill covers supplier energy charges, grid settlement/transmission charges, and pass-through ComEd utility distribution charges consolidated into a single monthly statement.
termination_for_cause: Non-payment of all charges may result in collection activities and contract termination per bill notice language
performance_obligations: Supply electricity at fixed price energy rate of $0.05632 per kWh through contract expiration in November 2026
Pass through utility (ComEd) distribution and regulatory charges accurately
Issue monthly statements with itemized charges
Maintain account portal at accounts.engieresources.com for payment and account management
Provide customer support Monday-Friday 7am-6pm CST at 866-693-6443
reporting_obligations: Monthly billing statement issued with meter read dates, usage history, and itemized charges
Carbon footprint equivalent reported monthly based on kWh consumption
End-of-year renewables statement available upon inquiry if renewables were purchased
Usage history by month (kWh per day average) provided on each statement
board_approval_required: Contract renewal or new contract execution prior to November 2026 expiration to avoid post-term month-to-month rates
Any decision to switch electricity suppliers or renegotiate supply terms before expiry
red_flags: No auto-renewal: contract expires 11/30/2026 and defaults to unspecified post-term month-to-month rate which could be significantly higher than contracted fixed rate — board must act before expiration
Late payment penalty of 1.5% per month (18% annualized) is aggressive and triggers immediately after due date
Non-payment explicitly noted as grounds for contract termination, which could disrupt facility operations
Underlying contract terms and conditions are not included in this document — key provisions such as early termination fees, force majeure, and dispute resolution are not visible for review
Distribution Facilities Charge of $2,971.95 is the single largest line item and is set by ComEd with no ability for NSIA to negotiate
Price changes allowed by law or regulatory actions may increase bill amounts without advance notice per bill language
Annual electricity cost estimated at approximately $176,700 based on current month — material expenditure that warrants board awareness
extraction_notes: This document is a monthly utility invoice, not the underlying supply contract. Key contract terms including original execution date, early termination provisions, pricing escalation clauses, full T&C, and renewal mechanics are not contained in this bill. The $176,722.80 annual value estimate is extrapolated from the single-month charge of $14,726.90 multiplied by 12 and should be verified against actual historical bills. Board should obtain and review the full ENGIE Resources supply contract before the November 2026 expiration. The contract expiration date of 11-30-2026 appears on the bill but the exact notice period required for renewal or termination is not stated here.

vendor_name: West Bend Insurance Company (formerly West Bend Mutual Insurance Company)
payment_terms: Premium of $38,204.00 total (including taxes, fees and surcharges); this declaration is not a bill — a separate billing invoice will be issued. Pro-rata refund if insurer cancels; less than pro-rata if insured cancels.
scope_summary: Commercial lines insurance policy (Policy No. A920490 03) issued by West Bend Insurance Company to North Shore Ice Arena, LLC covering the ice skating rink facility at 2111 Founders Dr, Northbrook IL 60062. Coverage includes Commercial Property ($17,546 premium), Commercial General Liability ($15,303 premium), Commercial Auto ($249 premium), and Commercial Liability Umbrella ($5,106 premium).
termination_for_cause: Insurer may cancel within first 60 days with 10 days notice for non-payment or 30 days notice for any other reason. After 60 days in effect, insurer may cancel only for: non-payment of premium (10 days notice), material misrepresentation, violation of policy terms, measurable increase in risk, loss of reinsurance, or regulatory directive (60 days notice for non-payment reasons). Named insured may cancel at any time by mailing advance written notice.
termination_for_convenience: Named insured may cancel at any time by mailing advance written notice to insurer; refund may be less than pro-rata. Insurer must provide 60 days written notice of non-renewal before policy expiration, stating reason; if insurer fails to provide proper non-renewal notice and insured obtains other insurance, policy ends on effective date of that other insurance.
performance_obligations: Maintain automatic sprinkler system (P-1) in complete working order as a condition of coverage; notify insurer of any suspension or impairment within 48 hours unless full protection can be restored within 48 hours
Pay all premiums when due; first Named Insured is solely responsible for premium payment
Provide prompt notice of loss or damage including description of property involved
Take all reasonable steps to protect covered property from further damage after a loss
Submit signed, sworn proof of loss within 60 days of insurer's request
Cooperate with insurer in investigation or settlement of any claim
Permit insurer to inspect property and examine books and records at any time during policy period and up to three years afterward
Resume business operations as quickly as possible following a covered loss (Business Income coverage condition)
Notify police if a law may have been broken in connection with a loss
Do not commit fraud, concealment, or material misrepresentation at any time
Policy rights and duties may not be transferred without insurer's written consent
reporting_obligations: Notify insurer promptly of any loss or damage with description of property involved
Report debris removal expenses to insurer in writing within 180 days of the date of direct physical loss or damage
Report pollutant clean-up expenses in writing within 180 days of the Covered Cause of Loss
Notify insurer of any suspension or impairment of protective safeguards (sprinkler system) unless full protection can be restored within 48 hours
Submit complete inventories of damaged and undamaged property at insurer's request
Submit signed, sworn proof of loss with information requested by insurer within 60 days of insurer's request
Report newly acquired property values to insurer to extend coverage beyond the 30-day automatic coverage window
If intending to continue business after loss, must resume operations as quickly as possible and report accordingly
board_approval_required: Policy transfer or assignment requires insurer written consent — board should be notified before any ownership restructuring that could affect policy validity

Three named insureds on policy (North Shore Ice Arena LLC, Techny Land Corporations, NSFP Club Sports Management & Consulting Group) — board should confirm all named insureds are appropriate and authorized
Amalgamated Bank of Chicago (30 N LaSalle St, 38th Floor, Chicago IL 60602) is listed as mortgageholder — board should confirm this mortgage relationship is current and properly authorized
Wind/Hail deductible of $50,000 and standard deductible of $10,000 represent significant self-insured retentions that board should be aware of for budget and reserve planning

90% coinsurance requirement on building ($11,150,400 limit), business personal property ($1,500,000 limit), and business income ($1,100,000 limit) — board should confirm values are adequate to avoid coinsurance penalty
Policy renewal or replacement decision should involve board notification given $38,204 annual premium obligation
red_flags: Three separate named insureds on one policy (NSIA LLC, Techny Land Corporations, NSFP Club Sports Management & Consulting Group) — unclear if all entities have insurable interest; could create coverage disputes or complicate claims
Cyber Incident Exclusion endorsement (CP1075) is listed — no affirmative cyber coverage appears in the policy; NSIA may have uninsured cyber exposure
Virus and Bacteria Exclusion (CP0140) explicitly excludes losses caused by virus or bacterium — pandemic-type events are not covered
Computer-related losses exclusion (IL0935Z) broadly excludes losses from computer hardware/software failure related to date recognition — scope may be broader than intended
Wind/Hail deductible of $50,000 is very high for an ice arena and could represent significant out-of-pocket exposure in a hail or wind event
Wind/Hail Cosmetic Damage Roof Surfacing is excluded — roof cosmetic damage from hail will not be covered even if structurally significant
90% coinsurance requirement creates significant underinsurance penalty risk if property values are not kept current; building insured at $11,150,400 but actual replacement cost should be verified

Insurer reserves right to seek reimbursement of defense costs if it later determines claim is not covered (Illinois Changes - Defense Costs endorsement IL0162) — this is a risk-shifting provision unfavorable to NSIA
Electronic data coverage is capped at $2,500 per policy year — very low limit for a business with point-of-sale, scheduling, and operational systems
Business Income coverage has a 72-hour waiting period before coverage begins — NSIA bears first 72 hours of business interruption loss
Protective Safeguards endorsement (CP0411) requires automatic sprinkler system (P-1) to be maintained; failure voids fire coverage — board must ensure sprinkler maintenance contracts are in place
No auto-renewal — policy must be actively renewed; 60-day non-renewal notice from insurer is required but NSIA should calendar renewal date proactively
Policy is not assignable without insurer written consent — relevant if NSIA undergoes any ownership or management restructuring
extraction_notes: Document is a complete commercial lines policy declaration with endorsements and coverage forms for policy period 05/28/2024 to 05/28/2025. Coverage limits for General Liability, Auto, and Umbrella sections are referenced by premium only in the declarations page — specific per-occurrence and aggregate limits are not shown in the extracted pages and would be in separate coverage part declarations not included in this document. Board should request the full CGL, Auto, and Umbrella declarations pages to confirm liability limits. The tentative rate endorsement (CP9993) is listed — board should confirm whether final rates have been set. NSFP Club Sports Management & Consulting Group is a named insured, which may require legal review to confirm appropriate insurable interest and whether this creates any coverage or liability implications under the NSIA operating agreement.

Named insureds include North Shore Ice Arena LLC, Techny Land Corporations, and NSFP Club Sports Management & Consulting Group.
termination_for_cause: Insurer may cancel mid-term after 60 days in force for: nonpayment of premium (10 days notice), material misrepresentation, violation of policy terms, measurable increase in risk, loss of reinsurance, or Director determination that continuation would violate insurance laws (60 days notice for non-payment reasons). Policy is also voidable for fraud, intentional concealment, or misrepresentation of material facts.
termination_for_convenience: First Named Insured may cancel at any time by mailing advance written notice to insurer; refund may be less than pro rata if insured-initiated. Insurer may cancel within first 60 days for any reason with 10 days notice (nonpayment) or 30 days notice (any other reason).
performance_obligations: Provide commercial property coverage up to $11,150,400 on building (replacement cost, 90% coinsurance, $10,000 deductible, $50,000 wind/hail deductible)
Provide business personal property coverage up to $1,500,000 (replacement cost, 90% coinsurance)
Provide business income and extra expense coverage up to $1,100,000 with 72-hour waiting period and 90-day extended period of indemnity
Provide commercial general liability coverage ($15,303 premium)
Provide commercial auto coverage ($249 premium)
Provide commercial liability umbrella coverage ($5,106 premium)
Provide equipment breakdown coverage (WB34 endorsement, $1,055 premium)
Pay covered losses within 30 days of receiving sworn proof of loss once agreement or appraisal award reached
Provide notice of intent not to renew at least 60 days before policy expiration
Notify mortgageholder (Amalgamated Bank of Chicago) of cancellation with at least 10 days notice (nonpayment) or 30 days notice (other reasons)
reporting_obligations: Insured must notify insurer promptly of any loss or damage with description of property involved
Insured must submit signed sworn proof of loss within 60 days of insurer's request
Insured must report debris removal expenses within 180 days of date of direct physical loss
Insured must report pollutant clean-up expenses within 180 days of covered cause of loss
Insured must notify insurer of any suspension or impairment of protective safeguards (automatic sprinkler system P-1 required at Location 1, Building 1)
Insured must notify police if a law may have been broken in connection with a loss
Insured must report newly acquired property values within 30 days of acquisition to maintain coverage extension
board_approval_required: Any policy changes or endorsements require insurer written consent and are only effective via endorsed endorsement — board should be notified before authorizing any coverage modifications
Transfer of policy rights or duties requires insurer written consent — board approval likely required before any assignment
Addition or removal of named insureds (currently includes Techny Land Corporations and NSFP Club Sports Management & Consulting Group) — board should be aware of and approve named insured schedule
Mortgageholder listed as Amalgamated Bank of Chicago — any changes to mortgage or lender arrangements should be reported to board and insurer
Annual premium of $38,204 constitutes a significant expenditure — board should confirm this is within approved budget authority
red_flags: NSFP Club Sports Management & Consulting Group is listed as a named insured — board should confirm this entity's relationship to NSIA and whether it is appropriate to extend coverage to this party under NSIA's policy
Techny Land Corporations is listed as a named insured — board should confirm ownership/landlord relationship and whether co-insuring this entity is intentional and appropriate
Cyber incident exclusion (CP1075) and computer-related losses exclusion (IL0935Z) leave NSIA with minimal cyber coverage — $2,500 cap on electronic data recovery is very low for an operating arena
Virus and bacteria exclusion (CP0140) means business income losses from pandemic-type events are not covered
Wind/hail deductible of $50,000 is substantially higher than standard $10,000 deductible — large out-of-pocket exposure for roof or exterior damage
Wind/hail cosmetic damage to roof surfacing is excluded entirely — roof replacement costs after hail may not be covered if only cosmetic

Coinsurance requirement of 90% on building ($11,150,400 limit) — if property is undervalued, insured faces significant penalty at time of loss; board should confirm current replacement cost appraisal supports this limit
Insurer reserves right to audit books and records during policy period and up to three years afterward — board should be aware of this audit right

Defense cost reimbursement clause (IL0162): if insurer initially defends a claim then determines no coverage, it may seek reimbursement of defense costs paid after issuing a reservation of rights — financial exposure for NSIA

Policy is nonassessable mutual membership policy — NSIA is technically a member of West Bend Mutual Holding Company with voting rights, which is an unusual feature of mutual insurance that board should note
No explicit auto-renewal provision identified — policy requires active renewal each year; lapse risk if renewal is not timely processed by Connor & Gallagher
extraction_notes: Document is a complete commercial lines policy declaration package with endorsements for policy period 05/28/2024 to 05/28/2025. Policy number A920490 03. The document was truncated after page 61 of the causes of loss special form; remaining endorsements including CGL, auto, and umbrella declarations and forms were not fully visible but premiums are captured in the summary declaration page. No management agreement or service contract terms are present — this is purely an insurance policy. Board should note the inclusion of NSFP Club Sports Management & Consulting Group and Techny Land Corporations as named insureds and confirm this is intentional. Recommend legal or insurance broker review of cyber exclusions given the operational dependence of an ice arena on refrigeration and building control systems.
_source_file: 2024-05-28 - North Shore Ice Arena Commercial Policy Declaration.pdf

payment_terms: 30% initial investment at agreement execution; balance due via progress payments per Article 4; Applications for Payment submitted by 25th of month, payment due by 25th of following month; 5% retainage withheld; final payment within 30 days of final Certificate for Payment; interest on late payments at 1.5% per month. Note: vendor's original proposal (Exhibit D) stated 50% at agreement, 45% prior to shipping, 5% prior to ice making — conflict with AIA form terms requires clarification.
scope_summary: Everything Ice, Inc. will replace the existing ice refrigeration system at North Shore Ice Arena (200x85x28 ft rink) by demolishing the existing mechanical equipment and installing a new halocarbon-based rink chiller system with variable-flow pump skid, all interconnecting piping, insulation, controls, and commissioning. The contractor will also provide training to arena personnel and the local service contractor upon system startup.
termination_for_cause: Owner may terminate upon 7 days written notice if contractor repeatedly fails to supply workers or materials, fails to pay subcontractors, repeatedly disregards laws/codes, or is guilty of substantial breach. Contractor may terminate upon 7 additional days notice if Owner fails to make payment or Architect fails to certify payment for 30 days, and may recover payment for work executed plus overhead, profit, and termination costs. Upon default by Owner, contractor has right after 7 days written notice to retake goods, hold and dispose of them, and collect deficiency.
termination_for_convenience: Owner may terminate at any time for convenience without cause and must pay contractor for work executed, costs incurred by reason of termination including subcontract termination costs, and any termination fee (fee amount left blank in agreement — not specified).
performance_obligations: Remove and dispose of existing primary refrigerant (134a) and existing mechanical equipment (chiller, pumps, condenser)
Drain and store existing secondary refrigerant (brine) for reuse
Remove existing piping deemed unsuitable for new system
Provide and install complete Everything Ice Rink Chiller halocarbon-based system with Vari-Pump variable flow system
Install all interconnecting piping, fluids, refrigerants, hangers, insulation, and control termination
Complete system pressure testing, adjustments, and commissioning
Provide training session to arena personnel and local service contractor
Complete all applicable working drawings required for the project
Submit construction schedule promptly after contract award and provide weekly construction updates
Provide Owner and Architect access to work at all times
Maintain site cleanliness and remove waste upon completion
Provide certificates of insurance prior to commencement and upon renewal
Notify Owner within 3 business days of any impending insurance cancellation or expiration
Notify Owner of subcontractors proposed for principal portions of work
Protect Owner from improper liens; remove any lien by payment or bond
Provide as-built construction documents, warranties, and O&M manuals at closeout
Provide daily supervision sign-off documentation (Exhibit F) when supervision services are provided
Not mobilize to site without authorized Pre-Mobilization Checklist (Exhibit E) from Owner
reporting_obligations: Submit construction schedule promptly after contract award for Owner and Architect approval
Provide weekly construction updates on manufacturing and installation schedule progress
Submit Applications for Payment by 25th of each month with supporting documentation
Notify Owner in writing upon achieving substantial completion; Owner then has 72 hours to provide punch list
Notify Owner and Architect promptly of any errors, inconsistencies, or omissions in Contract Documents
Notify Owner within 3 business days of impending or actual cancellation or expiration of required insurance
Notify Owner of proposed subcontractors for principal work portions as soon as practicable after award
Notify Owner of any nonconformity with applicable laws discovered during work
Notify Owner and Architect immediately upon encountering hazardous materials on site
Report deviations from schedule or contract documents to Architect during site visits
board_approval_required: Contract execution for $620,919 capital expenditure — likely requires board approval under NSIA operating agreement given contract value
Any Change Orders or Construction Change Directives that increase the Contract Sum above the original $620,919
Termination for convenience decision — significant financial and operational consequence requiring board awareness
Owner consent to assignment of contract to lender (Section 19 of Agreement)
Execution of UCC-1 financing statements in favor of Everything Ice, Inc. granting contractor security interest in equipment until paid in full
Approval of price escalation adjustments if material/labor/transportation costs increase more than 2% — contractor entitled to equitable adjustment
Decision to accept or reject contractor's proposed subcontractors for principal work portions
Any agreement to waive or modify retainage reduction terms
Securing Builder's Risk insurance as required under Article 17
red_flags: FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE (Addendum Article R): Contractor inserted Pennsylvania jurisdiction (Cambria County Common Pleas or W.D. Pa. federal court) overriding arbitration and Illinois venue — NSIA is in Illinois but would be forced to litigate in Pennsylvania; this directly conflicts with AIA form's Illinois governing law provision and is highly one-sided

UCC-1 FILING REQUIREMENT (Addendum Article N/F): Contractor retains title to all goods until full payment and requires Owner to execute UCC-1 financing statements before equipment delivery — creates a security interest in NSIA's equipment; board should be aware this encumbers the property

PRICE ESCALATION CLAUSE (Addendum Article 9B and Exhibit D): Contractor entitled to price increases exceeding 2% for material, labor, or transportation cost increases through no fault of contractor — open-ended cost exposure on a fixed-price contract

TERMINATION FEE LEFT BLANK: Article 20.3 (termination for convenience) references a termination fee but the amount or method is not filled in — creates ambiguity about NSIA's financial exposure upon convenience termination

PAYMENT TERMS CONFLICT: AIA form specifies 30% initial investment with progress payments, but Exhibit D (original proposal) states 50% at agreement, 45% prior to shipping, 5% prior to ice making — these are materially different and the controlling terms are unclear

ADDENDUM SUPREMACY CLAUSE: Addendum states its provisions take precedence over the primary AIA document — the addendum contains numerous contractor-favorable terms that override standard AIA owner protections

THIRD-PARTY SIGNATORIES: Agreement includes signature lines for Club Sports Consulting Group, Wilmette Hockey Association President, and Winnetka Hockey Club President — their legal role and authority in binding these entities or NSIA is unclear and requires legal review

OWNER ACTING AS ARCHITECT: Owner (NSIA) is designated as the Architect throughout the agreement, meaning NSIA must perform all Architect certification functions including payment certification — NSIA may lack expertise for these responsibilities and loses independent third-party oversight

INJUNCTIVE RELIEF CLAUSE (Addendum Article K): If Owner cannot demonstrate ability to pay, contractor is entitled to injunctive relief prohibiting continued use and deterioration of equipment — could result in arena shutdown

EQUIPMENT TITLE RETENTION (Exhibit F / Addendum N): Customer sign-off sheet confirms seller retains title until final payment and can remove equipment if payment not made — refrigeration system could be removed post-installation if payment dispute arises

MANDATORY SERVICE CONTRACT REQUIREMENT: Owner must enter into a long-term service contract with a qualified local refrigeration company as a condition of equipment guarantee — failure voids warranty; this creates an ongoing financial obligation not quantified in this contract

SCHEDULE FLEXIBILITY FOR CONTRACTOR: If Owner does not commence work within 7 days of a pre-arranged schedule, contractor may adjust schedule based on other work — could significantly delay project completion

CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES WAIVER: Both parties waive consequential damages including lost income, financing costs, and business losses — NSIA waives right to recover lost ice rental revenues if contractor delays cause arena downtime
NO LIQUIDATED DAMAGES: Section 3.5 references liquidated damages but none are specified — NSIA has no contractual remedy for delay damages beyond actual provable costs
MANUFACTURING LEAD TIME RISK: 18-23 week manufacturing plus 4-6 week installation timeline means substantial completion is approximately 22-29 weeks from commencement with no penalty for delay
BOND WAIVER (Addendum Article 17B): Addendum states contractor shall not be required to furnish any bond — removes standard AIA performance and payment bond protection for NSIA
CONCRETE TESTING COST SHIFT: Only one 28-day concrete test is included; additional testing requested by Owner triggers additional cost reimbursement to contractor
PREMATURE MOBILIZATION COSTS: If Owner's site is not ready per Exhibit E checklist and contractor arrives anyway, Owner bears all wasted crew expenses including wages, travel, and living costs
extraction_notes: Several pages of Addendum 1 (Pages 27-31) contain heavily redacted or blank fields where specific names, addresses, and certain commercial terms should appear but are missing or illegible. The Owner's representative name in Article 4B of the Addendum is blank. The exact termination fee for convenience termination is not specified. The document references Exhibit B (Scope of Work and Exclusion List), Exhibit C (Schedule of Values), Exhibit D (Original Sales Proposal), Exhibit E (Pre-Mobilization Checklist), and Exhibit F (Daily Supervision Sign-Off Sheet) — all partially provided but Exhibit D contains different payment terms (50/45/5) than the AIA form (30% initial investment), requiring legal reconciliation. The three additional signatories (Club Sports Consulting Group, Wilmette Hockey Association, Winnetka Hockey Club) require legal review to determine their contractual role and whether their signatures were obtained. Insurance limits for Professional Liability and Pollution Liability are left blank. The document should be reviewed by construction counsel familiar with Illinois law given the Pennsylvania forum selection clause conflict. No completion date is specified — only manufacturing and installation duration ranges are provided.
_source_file: 2026-01-30 - AIA Standard Contractor Agreement Form Template.pdf
